text: Robotic lobectomy is performed through three VATS incisions.
The first is a camera incision through the 7th or 8th interspace.
The second is a 9th or 10th interspace incision posteriorly.
The lung is retracted and a utility incision is made either at the level of the 4th or 5th interspace depending on which lobe is being done.
Here on the left side, a left upper lobectomy is going to be done and therefore the incision is at the level of the superior vein.
The robotic instruments are then docked, usually through the utility incision.
On the left side, a Cartier forcep is introduced.
The camera is introduced through the camera port, and the last incision is used for the spatula, seen here.
This shows docking of the surgical cart for a right-sided VADS procedure.
In this example, the incisions have been made using conventional thoracoscopy.
The surgical card is brought in at an angled approach from the posterior aspect of the patient at roughly 45 to 60 degrees.
The first trocar to be hooked up is a camera port in the eighth or ninth intercostal space in the posterior axillary line.
The da Vinci scope can then be set to help guide placement of the other trocars.

One trocar can be placed through the utility incision in the midpoint of the incision under direct and thoracoscopic vision and the posterior trocar in a similar fashion can be placed through the posterior incision with the trocar already attached to the instrument arm.
It is important to be able to visualize this thoracoscopically prior to placement of the robotic instruments.
Once the trocars are in the chest,
In the posterior incision, typically a grasping forceps such as a Cartier forceps can be placed and positioned in the midpoint of the chest under thoracoscopic guidance.
In a similar fashion through the utility incision, a cutting type of instrument such as a Cartier forceps, hook cautery or scissors with cautery can be placed through the utility incision.
The assistant will stand at the anterior aspect of the patient and help with retraction through the utility incision.
Once all the instruments have been placed, the operating surgeon is then free to move to the surgeon's console to be able to begin the robotic portion of the procedure.
